concept.title: General Questions
concept.shortdesc: This section answers general questions about metR.

othermeta.name: shortdesc
othermeta.content: Provides a high-level overview of the metR platform, its purpose, and the problems it is designed to solve.

section.title: What is metR?

p.b: metR turns scattered documents into structured, AI-ready knowledge you can trust.

section.p: Instead of just converting files from one format to another, metR transforms legacy content into clean, governed, reusable building blocks that scale across products, teams, and regions.
The result is content that’s easier to manage, faster to publish, safer to audit, and immediately usable for modern AI initiatives like semantic search, RAG, and knowledge graphs.

section.title: Who is metR built for?

p.b: metR is built for organizations where content accuracy, speed, and compliance directly impact revenue, risk, or reputation.

section.p: That includes product-led companies, regulated enterprises, and platforms preparing documentation for AI-driven experiences.
It aligns the needs of executives (risk and ROI), product leaders (speed to market), and content strategists (structure and reuse) in one unified system.

section.title: How does metR reduce operational risk?

p.b: metR makes content changes visible, traceable, and auditable.

section.p: Every transformation preserves structure and meaning, with validation before content is reused or published.
This dramatically reduces the risk of outdated instructions, inconsistent outputs, or untraceable changes—especially critical in regulated or high-impact environments.

section.title: How is metR different from other document conversion software in the market?

p.b: metR is not a typical document conversion tool.

section.p: Instead of only changing file formats, it processes content in a way that preserves meaning, structure, and relationships. Documents are converted into structured, reusable components that are easier to find and manage, with appropriate review, validation, and governance applied before and after conversion.
Once validated, this structured content can be used directly to support AI capabilities such as smart search, RAG systems, and knowledge graphs without extensive additional rework.

section.title: How does converting to DITA make information more accessible?

p.b: DITA breaks large documents into small, structured topics with a clear purpose.

section.p: This makes information easier to find, reuse, update, and deliver consistently across different outputs and systems.

section.title: Where can I find user testimonials or case studies about metR's effectiveness?

p.b: metR shares industry examples and use-case overviews in its documentation.

section.p: Detailed case studies and customer references are available through demos, pilots, or direct engagement with the metR team. For more information,
Contact us or book a demo, we’ll map a pilot with measurable success metrics.
